summary: Full backend + frontend e2e testing of dontworkhere.xyz. Backend: 22/22 pytest cases passed against the public preview URL covering public entries, search, slug lookup + 404, submission validation (short quote, missing/invalid source URL), auth guard (401 without/invalid token), moderator stats/list/approve/edit/unpublish/reject flows, and moderator management (add/remove, self-remove guard). Frontend: Home (hero copy, search debouncing filters live to 'sleep' returning 2 cards, card click navigates to detail), EntryDetail (quote/sources/share visible, invalid slug shows not-found state), Submit (add/remove dynamic sources, client validation blocks <20 char quote, valid submission shows 'Your Red Flag has been raised!' confirmation), ModLogin shows 'Continue with Google', ProtectedRoute redirects unauthenticated /mod/dashboard to /mod/login, seeded session cookie loads dashboard with 4 stat cards + 4 tabs (pending/approved/rejected/moderators), approve+score=5 moves entry from Pending to Live, add moderator + remove moderator both succeed. No business-logic bugs found.
action_items: (Doc nit only) The review-brief example slug 'boss-receipts' does not match any seeded entry — actual seeded slugs follow the {person}-{company}-{year} pattern (e.g. 'gwen-castellano-maximal-output-inc-2024'). This is an expected slug-generation outcome, not a bug; tests were updated to use the first real slug dynamically. If a canonical 'boss-receipts' slug was intended (e.g., for marketing/share URLs in the prompt), seed an entry with that slug explicitly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 445 lines and still single-file — fine for now but split into routes/auth/models modules before it grows much further.
GET /api/mod/entries returns full Mongo docs (including submitter_email) — fine for moderators, but consider an explicit projection to avoid accidental leakage if endpoint ever becomes broader.
POST /api/mod/moderators silently reactivates a soft-deleted moderator without surfacing that change to the caller — consider returning a flag like {ok:true, reactivated:true} for audibility.
DELETE /api/mod/moderators/{email} matches by raw path param without any 404 on unknown email — currently returns 200 even if the email doesn't exist; consider 404 for clarity.
Frontend api client base URL is centralized in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js (confirmed via grep) — good; all pages use it. data-testid coverage is excellent across all interactive elements.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Home, EntryDetail, Submit, ModLogin, Protected redirect, Dashboard tabs/approve/moderator add/remove all verified)

test_credentials: Moderator: qa.mod@example.com / session_token=test_session_qa (seeded in test_database per /app/auth_testing.md). Backend accepts the same token via Authorization: Bearer header or session_token cookie.
seed_data_creation: Seeded a moderator (qa.mod@example.com) + user_sessions row with token test_session_qa for auth-gated tests. Created and cleaned up TEST_ prefixed entries and uitest_*@example.com moderators after tests; original 8 sample entries restored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the regression baseline (22 tests, ~5s). Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://boss-receipts.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Seeded auth: mongosh test_database -> moderators+user_sessions per /app/auth_testing.md. Real seeded slugs follow {person}-{company}-{year} format — do NOT assume 'boss-receipts' exists. Dashboard tab keys are: pending/approved/rejected/moderators (not 'live').
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The only discrepancy noted was the brief's example slug 'boss-receipts' not matching seeded data — root cause: brief used a placeholder slug; seed.py generates slugs via slugify(person-company-year). Mitigation: tests now resolve a real slug dynamically from GET /api/entries.
